Human Vulnerability PersonasAssignment 3Data Pipeline DocumentationSYNTHETIC PERSONASMADISON — DATA PIPELINESatwika MaddukuriBranding & AIINFO 7375Northeastern University Project OverviewHuman Vulnerability Personas is a data pipeline built on Madison's Synthetic Personas component. It addresses a critical gap — human behavior causes 80% of security breaches, yet no existing framework models it.This pipeline collects 270 clean records from 3 sources — phishing emails, social engineering interactions, and live government advisories — to build 4 personas: The Clicker, The Password Recycler, The Shadow IT User, and The Over-Trusted Insider.These personas serve both security teams for tabletop exercises and marketing teams for brand trust campaigns. Data Inventory: Source 1Source 1: Phishing Email Dataset (Kaggle)— a balanced set of real phishing and legitimate emails labeled for detection. Type:Labeled phishing and legitimate email recordsAmount:120 records Purpose:Captures how phishing emails exploit human urgency and trust — feeds The Clicker and Password Recycler personas Collection method:Downloaded CSV from Kaggle, loaded via n8n Read File node, extracted with Extract from File node, limited to 120 recordsQuality:120/120 complete records — no missing fields, no duplicates Data Inventory: Source 2Source 2: Global Cognitive Threat Dataset (Kaggle)— real social engineering interactions across email, SMS, WhatsApp and Telegram from 249 countries. Type:Real social engineering interactions across email, SMS, WhatsApp and TelegramAmount:120 records Purpose:Captures real manipulation tactics and persuasion strategies — feeds Shadow IT User and Over-Trusted Insider personas Collection method:Downloaded CSV from Kaggle, loaded via n8n Read File node, extracted with Extract from File node, limited to 120 from 35,574 availableQuality:120/120 complete records — no missing fields, no duplicates Data Inventory: Source 3Source 3: CISA Cybersecurity Advisories RSS Feed —real-time threat intelligence directly from the US government. Type:Live government cybersecurity advisory dataAmount:30 records Purpose:Grounds personas in real current threats — connects human behavior patterns to active cybersecurity advisories Collection method:Live HTTP request to CISA.gov RSS feed, parsed XML using custom JavaScript Code node in n8n, limited to 30 recordsQuality:30/30 complete records — no missing fields, no duplicates Data Quality StatisticsAll 270 records collected are clean, complete and ready for Assignment 4.Source Records Missing Fields DuplicatesPhishing Dataset 120 None NoneGCT Dataset 120 None NoneCISA RSS Feed 30 None NoneTotal 270 None NoneFile: human_vulnerability_personas_data.csvSize: 106 KB Setup GuideWhat you need:Node.js v24+ and n8n (latest version)Installation:• Download and install Node.js from nodejs.org• Open Command Prompt (Windows) or Terminal (Mac) and run npm install -g n8n• Run n8n start• Open your browser and go to http://localhost:5678To run the workflow:• Import Maddukuri_Satwika_A3_Workflow.json into n8n• Place both CSV files in C:\Users\[yourname]\.n8n-files\ (Windows) or ~/.n8n-files/ (Mac)• Click the orange Execute workflow button• Output saved automatically to human_vulnerability_personas_data.csv Workflow Architecture: 3 Parallel Branches Branch 1: Phishing Email Pipeline— Reads thephishing email CSV (800 records) and limits to 120.Branch 2: Social Engineering Pipeline— Reads theGCT social engineering CSV (35,574 records) andlimits to 120.Branch 3: Live Threat Intelligence— Fetches liveadvisories from the CISA RSS feed, parses the XMLusing a custom code node, and limits to 30.All 3 branches merge into 270 clean records,converted to CSV and saved ashuman_vulnerability_personas_data.csv (106 KB). Strategic Impact: The Human Perimeter The ProblemDespite billions spent on firewalls, encryption and threat detection, 80% of breaches still trace back to human behavior — a click, a weak password, a misplaced trust. Technology secures systems. Humans create the gaps. The SolutionThis data pipeline builds the foundation for a new layer of security intelligence — one that models the human perimeter. By understanding how The Clicker, The Password Recycler, The Shadow IT User and The Over-Trusted Insider behave, organizations can close the gap technology alone cannot. The ImpactSecurity teams run smarter tabletop exercises. Marketing teams design campaigns that build genuine digital trust. The result: security that is not just strong — but human-aware. Conclusion: Securing the FutureThis pipeline collects, processes, and merges 270 clean records across 3 sources into a single structured dataset, ready to power the PersonaBuilder Agent in Assignment 4What was built:A fully automated n8n workflow collecting phishing, social engineering and threat advisory data What comes next:Assignment 4 — using this data to generate 4 Human Vulnerability Personas that serve both security and brand teamsThe bigger picture:Security knowledge is a branding superpower. This project proves it.

Human Risk Report Generator Demo Walkthrough Assignment 4 · INFO 7375 · Branding & AI Satwika Maddukuri · Northeastern University Built with n8n + Gemini 2.5 Flash June 2026 1. Problem & Solution THE PROBLEM 80% of security breaches trace back to human behavior — yet no existing GRC framework models it. Frameworks like NIST 800-53, ISO 27001, and FFIEC evaluate technical controls exclusively. They produce no model of how employees actually behave around security, data access, and compliance requirements. This leaves a critical blind spot in every vendor risk report produced. THE SOLUTION Human Risk Report Generator extends Madison's Synthetic Personas component to generate AI-powered Human Risk Reports. The system analyzes 270 real cybersecurity records and uses Gemini 2.5 Flash to generate tailored reports for any company type — with a Human Risk Score, 4 persona profiles, attack scenarios, and brand trust recommendations. Key data insight: avg fear_trigger_score of 0.523 across 270 records — fear-based attacks are the highest risk vector, directly informing persona risk scores across all 10 company types. THE BUSINESS VALUE "$5,000 consulting report generated in 45 seconds for $0.002 — powered by 270 real cybersecurity records" 2. Assignment 3 — Data Pipeline Foundation WHAT WAS IN ASSIGNMENT 3 Assignment 3 built the data pipeline that powers this project. It collected 270 clean records from 3 real-world sources using an automated n8n workflow with 3 parallel branches. All records were merged into a single structured CSV file: human_vulnerability_personas_data.csv (106KB). Assignment 4 takes this data and adds AI intelligence on top. SOURCE 1 — PHISHING EMAIL DATASET (KAGGLE) Type Labeled phishing and legitimate email records Amount 120 records (from 800 available) Purpose Captures how phishing emails exploit human urgency and trust — feeds The Clicker and Password Recycler personas Collection Method Downloaded CSV from Kaggle, loaded via n8n Read File node, extracted with Extract from File node, limited to 120 records Quality 120/120 complete records — no missing fields, no duplicates SOURCE 2 — GLOBAL COGNITIVE THREAT DATASET (KAGGLE) Type Real social engineering interactions across email, SMS, WhatsApp and Telegram from 249 countries Amount 120 records (from 35,574 available) Purpose Captures real manipulation tactics and persuasion strategies — feeds Shadow IT User and Over-Trusted Insider personas Collection Method Downloaded CSV from Kaggle, loaded via n8n Read File node, extracted with Extract from File node, limited to 120 records Quality 120/120 complete records — no missing fields, no duplicates SOURCE 3 — CISA CYBERSECURITY ADVISORIES RSS FEED Type Live government cybersecurity advisory data from the US Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency Amount 30 records (live feed) Purpose Grounds personas in real current threats — connects human behavior patterns to active cybersecurity advisories Collection Method Live HTTP request to CISA.gov RSS feed, parsed XML using custom JavaScript Code node in n8n, limited to 30 records Quality 30/30 complete records — no missing fields, no duplicates 3. Assignment 3 — n8n Workflow Architecture 3 PARALLEL BRANCHES — HOW DATA WAS COLLECTED The Assignment 3 workflow runs 3 parallel branches simultaneously, each collecting from a different source. All 3 branches merge into a single output of 270 clean records. BRANCH 1 BRANCH 2 BRANCH 3 📧 Phishing Email Read File node → Extract from File → Limit to 120 🌐 GCT Dataset Read File node → Extract from File → Limit to 120 🏛 CISA RSS HTTP Request → Parse CISA XML → Limit to 30 120 records ✅ 120 records ✅ 30 records ✅ ↓ All 3 branches merge into Merge All Sources node → Save Final CSV → Export to CSV → human_vulnerability_personas_data.csv (106KB, 270 records, 50 columns) ASSIGNMENT 3 OUTPUT — WHAT WAS COLLECTED The final output of Assignment 3 was a single clean CSV file with 270 records and 50 columns including: id, subject, sender, email_text, has_link, has_attachment, urgency_flag, label, sentiment_score, urgency_score, authority_claim, fear_trigger_score, trust_manipulation_score, cognitive_load_score, persuasion_strategy, social_engineering_pattern, attack_type, attack_stage, attack_complexity, and more. KEY DATASET STATISTICS USED IN ASSIGNMENT 4 METRIC VALUE USED IN A4 avg urgency_score 0.499 ✅ In Gemini prompt avg fear_trigger_score 0.523 ✅ In every report insight avg trust_manipulation_score 0.476 ✅ In Gemini prompt avg cognitive_load_score 0.494 ✅ In Gemini prompt avg CMS (Composite Manipulation) 0.508 ✅ In Gemini prompt Top attack type Phishing (38 records) ✅ Informs persona scores Top persuasion strategy Scarcity (28 records) ✅ Informs attack scenarios Top channel Chat (47 records) ✅ Informs attack vectors Screenshot: Assignment 3 n8n canvas showing 3 parallel branches — Phishing Dataset branch, GCT Dataset branch, CISA Security Feed branch — all merging into Merge All Sources node 4. Data Foundation & Personas DATA SOURCES — 270 CLEAN RECORDS The pipeline collects data from 3 sources via n8n workflow automation: • Phishing Email Dataset (Kaggle) — 120 records of labeled phishing and legitimate emails • Global Cognitive Threat Dataset (Kaggle) — 120 records of social engineering interactions across email, SMS, WhatsApp and Telegram from 249 countries • CISA Cybersecurity Advisories RSS Feed — 30 live government threat intelligence records 270 RECORDS ANALYZED 10 REPORTS GENERATED 45s PER REPORT $0.002 COST PER RUN 4 HUMAN VULNERABILITY PERSONAS PERSONA RISK TYPE DATA PATTERN 🖱 The Clicker Phishing Risk High urgency/fear trigger emails, low complexity attacks 🔑 Password Recycler Credential Risk Authority manipulation, OTP fraud, trust exploitation 📱 Shadow IT User Compliance Risk Chat/SMS channels, social engineering, medium complexity 🔓 Over-Trusted Insider Access Risk High trust manipulation, extraction stage, high complexity 5. Assignment 4 — n8n Workflow Overview COMPLETE NODE PIPELINE The workflow runs 6 nodes in sequence — all showing green checkmarks on successful execution: • Merge All Sources — combines 270 records from 3 data sources • Save Final CSV — converts merged data to CSV format • Export to CSV — writes human_vulnerability_personas_data.csv (106KB) • Gemini AI Analysis — HTTP POST to gemini-2.5-flash with dataset summary and company type prompt • Parse Gemini Response — Code node extracts and parses JSON from AI response • Generate HTML Report — builds complete HTML report with risk scores, personas, and recommendations Screenshot: Full n8n canvas showing all 6 nodes with green checkmarks — Merge All Sources → Save Final CSV → Export to CSV → Gemini AI Analysis → Parse Gemini Response → Generate HTML Report 6. AI Intelligence in Action PART 1 — WHAT THE AI DOES The Gemini AI Analysis node receives a structured prompt containing the 270-record dataset summary and a company type input. Gemini 2.5 Flash then: • Analyzes dataset statistics (avg fear_trigger_score 0.523, avg urgency_score 0.499, avg CMS 0.508) • Decides which personas are highest risk for the specific company type • Generates a risk score (0-100) and risk level (Critical/High/Medium/Low) • Produces 3 company-specific attack scenarios and 3 brand trust recommendations • Returns a key insight citing actual dataset statistics Screenshot: Gemini AI Analysis node output showing risk_score: 82, risk_level: High, all 4 personas with scores and reasons — green checkmark confirming successful AI response IMPROVEMENT OVER ASSIGNMENT 3 ASSIGNMENT 3 — BEFORE ASSIGNMENT 4 — AFTER Raw data collection only. Output: CSV file. No analysis, no insights, no actionable output. AI analyzes 270 records. Output: professional HTML report with risk scores, personas, attack scenarios and brand recommendations. 7. Error Handling 3 FAILURE PATHS COVERED The workflow implements error handling at multiple levels to ensure it never crashes: ⚠ API RATE LIMIT (429) Retry On Fail is ON in Gemini node settings. Max 3 retries with 5 second wait between tries. Execution continues even if all retries fail — workflow never stops. ⚠ MALFORMED JSON try/catch block in Parse Gemini Response code node. Strips emojis and special characters before parsing. Extracts JSON between { } brackets. Returns fallback defaults with real data if parse fails. ⚠ SERVICE UNAVAILABLE Error branch is active on the Gemini node (On Error: Continue). Fallback report with default values is returned. Workflow never crashes or stops regardless of AI service status. Screenshot: Gemini AI Analysis node Settings tab showing Retry On Fail: ON, Max Tries: 3, Wait Between Tries: 5000ms, On Error: Continue — and the Success/Error branch split visible on the canvas
